3.happening: We create seen as an empty map from character to its latest index.
3.why: Knowing where a character last appeared lets us jump the window instantly on a repeat.
3.invariant: seen holds the most recent index of every character encountered so far.

4.happening: The left edge starts at index 0.
4.why: The window begins empty at the front of the string.
4.invariant: Every character between left and right is currently unique.

5.why: No window has been measured yet.
5.invariant: best is the length of the longest repeat-free window seen so far.

6.happening: The right edge advances to the next character.
6.why: Extending right tries to grow the window by one more character.
6.invariant: right marks the newest character being added to the window.

7.happening: We read ch, the character at the right edge.
7.why: We must decide whether ch already lives inside the current window.
7.invariant: ch is the character at index right.

8.happening: We check whether ch is already inside the window.
8.why: A repeat inside the window would violate the no-duplicate rule.
8.invariant: The window may contain a duplicate only until this check fixes it.

9.happening: ch repeats inside the window, so left jumps to just past the earlier copy.
9.why: Skipping ahead is the fastest way to make the window valid again without rechecking every character.
9.invariant: After the jump, no character repeats between left and right.

10.happening: We record that ch was last seen at index right.
10.why: A future repeat of ch will need this position to know where to jump.
10.invariant: seen[ch] always holds the most recent index of ch.

11.happening: We compute window as right - left + 1.
11.why: The window length is what we are trying to maximize.
11.invariant: window counts the characters currently between left and right inclusive.

12.happening: We compare the current window against the best so far.
12.why: Only a longer window can improve the answer.

13.happening: This window is the longest yet, so best becomes {window}.
13.why: The answer is the maximum window length over the whole scan.
13.invariant: best equals the longest repeat-free substring found up to here.

14.why: Every window has been considered, so the maximum is final.
14.invariant: best is the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

init.happening: Set up the map, the left edge, and the best length.
init.why: The window needs a starting position and memory of seen characters.
init.invariant: The window is empty and no length has been recorded.

loop.happening: Advance the right edge to the next character.
loop.why: The window grows rightward one character per iteration.
loop.invariant: right is the newest position under consideration.

update.happening: Record the character and measure the window.
update.why: Each step refreshes the last-seen index and the current length.
update.invariant: The map and window length always describe the live window.

check.happening: Test for a repeat or a new record length.
check.why: These decisions keep the window valid and track the maximum.
check.invariant: A window is only counted once it is free of duplicates.

move.happening: Slide the left edge forward past a duplicate.
move.why: Jumping restores the no-repeat property in constant time.
move.invariant: After moving, the window contains distinct characters only.

return.invariant: The returned value is the maximum repeat-free length.
